page: Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, delivering 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache. Memory configuration consists of 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-capacity, low-latency local disk access. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The hardware lacks dedicated GPU accelerators, meaning all graphical and parallel computing tasks rely entirely on the CPU.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The CPU's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to top-tier performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. However, single-core performance presents a relative tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core results falling in the average middle 50% tier, despite Geekbench single-core scoring in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large block read operations rank in the top 10%, while write operations fall into the strong top 25% tier.

The c8id.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier efficiency for data-intensive and compute-heavy workloads. It achieves top 10% performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and database operations, making it highly suitable for large-scale database hosting and high-throughput web serving. Additionally, the presence of AMX instructions enables top-tier LLM inference speeds for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds for smaller models drop to average levels. Given the high density of compute cores, DDR5 memory, and substantial local NVMe storage, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-throughput local I/O and parallel processing without the need for discrete GPU hardware.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. While single-core performance sits in the average tier for some benchmarks, its multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations rank in the top 10% tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, which drive top-tier performance in cryptography, physics simulations, and LLM prompt processing. With a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and no integrated GPUs, this instance is designed for high-throughput parallel workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform combining high-density dedicated Intel Xeon cores with massive local NVMe SSD storage for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz.
256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory.
7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access.
Top-tier multi-core, database, and Redis benchmark performance.
Hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets.
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and LLM prompt processing.
